content_en:
% Fora more detailed biography of al-Hafiz Ibn al-Jawzi, refer to: Ibn Athir’s a/- Kamil, 12/71, Sibt Ibn al-Jawzi: Mirat al-Zamén, 8/481, al-Mundhisi: al-Takmila, Biography 608, al-Bagghal: a/-Mashyakha, 140, Aba Shamma: Thail al-Randatain, 21, Ibn al-Sai: a-Jami', 9/65, Ibn Khalkan: a/-Wafaydt, 3/140, al-Dhahabi: a/- ‘bar, 4/297, Diwal al-Islam, 2/19, Tathkirat al-Huffaz, 4/1342, Siyar al-A Var alNubula’, 21/365, Ibn Kathir: al-Bidayab wa’l-Nihayah, 13/26, Yon Rajab: Thail Tabagét al-Hanabila, 1/399, al-Jazati: Ghayat wa'l-Nihayab, 1/375, Siddiq Hasan Khan: a/:Taj al Mukallal, 70, Ibn Shatti: Mukbtasar Tabagat al-Hanabila, p. 42.

Know that all that Allah has allocated for humankind is for the purpose of benefiting them; either to bring forth a benefit such as the desire for food, or to prevent a harm such as the capability of anger. That said, when the desire for food is in excess it becomes gluttony and hence, harmful. On a similar note anger when imbalanced within a person becomes mischievous.

The purpose of this book is to employ the law of soundness and correctness through the channels of the mind and the heart, and to constrain Hawa so that it does not cause harm, and to treat Hawa that has been acted upon yet opposes the law of soundness and correctness. For this reason, I have divided this book into thirty chapters.

The Virtue of the Mind

of the mind and its location , subsequently this subject

has been extensively discussed. In addition many hadiths have been narrated establishing the excellence of the mind. Some of which I have already mentioned in my book “Dispraise of Desires”, therefore I will not repeat them here rather I will mention a selection:

P EOPLE HAVE DIFFERED REGARDING the essence

The excellence of a thing is known by its fruits, and from among the fruits of the mind is knowing the Creator, may He be exalted. For the mind has contemplated the proofs [of the existence of the Creator] until it knew Him, and has contemplated the proofs of the truthfulness of the Prophets until it recognized their truthfulness. The mind has encouraged obedience to Allah and to His messengers. It has schemed to get all that is difficult to obtain, and it tamed the animals. It taught man to build ships on which he reached what the sea had prevented men from reaching, and tricked the birds of the sea until they were hunted. All of this while man’s eyes observe the consequences and work in accordance with safety

and need. Furthermore it taught man to relinquish the life of this world for the hereafter. Because of it, human beings have excellence over animals that have been prevented from having it (the mind), and it is what qualifies man for the speech of Allah and His commandments. With it, man reaches the highest ranks that his kind can reach from the good of this world and the hereafter, in both knowledge and actions.

These virtues suffice the need to delve into detail.

Dispraise of Hawa (desires)

AWA IS THE INCLINATION OF one’s nature to what He: it and that should not be criticised when what is

sought after is lawful. However, it should be dispraised when one is excessive in following Hawa. That said, when Hawa is criticised in an absolute manner, it is because most of it is either

impermissible, or because people usually interpret lawfulness, and hence indulge in it in excess.

Know that part of the inner self is intellectual, the virtue of which is wisdom, the vice of which is ignorance, part of it is elicited, the virtue of which is poignancy, the vice of which is cowardice, part of it is lustful, the virtue of which is chastity and the vice of which is unrestrained Hawa. Exhibiting patience in the face of vice is a merit of the inner self by which a person endures both goodness and evil. Therefore, whoever lacks patience and allows his //awd to lead his mind has then made the follower be followed and the led a leader. That said, it is expected that everything he desires will return to him and that he will be harmed from where he expected to benefit, he will be saddened by that which he expected happiness to come from.

Indeed human beings are favored over beastly animals with the mind (intelligence) that is designated to restrain one from following Hawa, therefore when a person does not accept the judgment of his mind and abides by the judgment of his 7Yawa, the beastly animal becomes bettet than him. From the indicative signs through which the excellence of contradicting Hawa is proven is the honoring and the superiority of hunting dogs over other dogs, which is due to their ability to contradict their Hawd and to keep what they hunt for their master out of fear of punishment or as a show of appreciation.

Know that the example of Haw is like rampant flowing water leading the ship of one’s nature. A person of sound mind should comprehend, enduring hardship when contradicting his Hawa is much easier, than enduring what comes from following it; for the least to be expected when one follows his Hand is being in a state in which one neither can enjoy it nor possess the ability to prevent oneself from following it, becoming accustomed to something allows it to become an addiction; such as those addicted to sexual intercourse or alcohol.

Contemplating these matters makes it easier for a person to reject his Hawa. From amongst what makes Hawa despicable to a person is the deep reflection of oneself, upon which one will deduce that he was not created to act in conformity with his Hawa. For a camel eats more than he does, a bird copulates more than him, and the desires of animals are unrestricted and they do not experience regret afterwards. Thus when [knowing that] the share of humans from desires is never ultimate, and when those desires were spoiled with [human’s] imperfections, accordingly humankind should know that [all these signs indicate that] they were not created to follow theit own Hawa, notwithstanding, the dispraised

Dispraise of Hawa (desires)

Hawa is, that which exceeds the limits and it is that which the mind judges as faulty, as I have already elucidated. Therefore whatever you desire of which there is a necessity to possess and that which assists the self to improve is praised rather than dispraised.

The Difference Between the Perspective of Mind and the Perspective of Hawa

ure without taking into consideration its consequences,

though one might know that this satisfaction will bring annoyance that actually exceeds it [exceeds the satisfaction] and also prevents one from attaining other similar satisfaction. Nonetheless Hawa turns one away from reflecting on all this, which makes one lower to the exact state of animals, however animals are excused because they do not observe the consequences of what they do. Hence, a wise person should not descend from a high and noble state that he is honored with, to the state of those that are low [animals], the mind should observe the consequences {of actions] one should examine the benefits and interests, since it acts as a resolute person and an advisory doctor.

| q NOW THAT HAWA CALLS TO ATTAINING pleas-

The similitude of Hand is that of an ignorant boy and a gluttonous sick person. For this reason, when the mind perceives something that differs to the Hawa’s entreaty, a wise and reasonable person should then consult his mind, particularly as he is

The Difference Between the Perspective of Mind and the Perspective...

aware that it is knowledgeable as well sincere in its advice, he should be patient upon what the mind orders him to do, because knowing the excellence of the mind is enough for him to favor it. Further if he needs additional proof of the uprightness of the mind’s decision, he should contemplate the consequences of following his Hawa such as scandalous exposure in front of people, vilification, missing virtues and good deeds. For this glory has ever deteriorated, honor has been disgraced and humiliated or a bird hunted except as a result of following Hawa?!

The proof can also be found when a person anticipates what his Hawd is calling him to prior to attaining it, he should then reflect on his state after his satisfaction has passed, he should compare this between his satisfaction and his sin for that is when he will know that he has lost double of what he has gained.

A poet said regarding this:

How much satisfaction that provoked happiness, Ended up revealing sadness and sorrows

How many desites ripped from their participants The garment of religion and virtue

Know that when a human acts in accordance to his Hawa, he will find in himself disgrace and humiliation, even if this Hawa does not harm him, that is because he feels subdued by his Hawa, but when he overcomes his Hawd he will find in himself honor and pride as he will feel triumphant and victorious. For this reason, when people see an ascetic they become amazed by him, and they would kiss his hand [to show respect] because they perceive him as a person who is strong enough to leave what they were weak in the face of and that is disobeying Hawa.

Averting Passionate Love (Yshq)

whether in their bodies, religion or both; a disease for which

Ihave composed the book ‘Dispravse of Hawa’,wherein I have listed enough cures for “shg, nonetheless, I will mention some of these cures here so as not to leave out anything that this book was intended for, therefore, I say:

I SHQ IS A DISEASE THAT HAS RUINED many people

Whoever abstains from the amalgamation [of harmful elements causing illness] by lowering his gaze, will be saved from this disease, otherwise he will be as much diseased as he who amalgamates in such harmful acts.

If he redresses this before it becomes deeply rooted, the cure might benefit him. However if he leaves it until it becomes deeply rooted the cure will be of no benefit. Know that the mete looking at what is coveted does not result in %shg, rather looking at the coveted often increases as it is aided and supported by the strength of greed, youthfulness and lust. Therefore whoever wants the cure should hasten to it before this illness becomes deeply rooted and that is by blocking the means leading to it (lowering the gaze)

Averting Passionate Love (‘Ishq)

and by enduring it with patience. For indeed self-restraint and strength are the best of cures. The best of aids in this is fear of Allah, restraining the disdainful self from the state of humiliation and remembering the inner flaws of the loved one. As Ibn Mas‘td said: “If any of you like a woman, he should remember her flaws.”
content_id:
% Untuk biografi lebih lengkap tentang al-Hafiz Ibn al-Jawzi, lihat: Ibn Athir dalam a/-Kamil, 12/71, Sibt Ibn al-Jawzi: Mirat al-Zamān, 8/481, al-Mundhiri: al-Takmila, Biografi 608, al-Bagghal: al-Mashyakha, 140, Abu Shamma: Thail al-Randatain, 21, Ibn al-Sa'i: al-Jami', 9/65, Ibn Khalkan: al-Wafayāt, 3/140, al-Dhahabi: al-'Ibar, 4/297, Diwal al-Islam, 2/19, Tathkirat al-Huffaz, 4/1342, Siyar al-A'lām al-Nubalā', 21/365, Ibn Kathir: al-Bidāyah wa'l-Nihāyah, 13/26, Ibn Rajab: Thail Tabaqāt al-Hanābila, 1/399, al-Jazari: Ghāyat wa'l-Nihāyah, 1/375, Siddiq Hasan Khan: al-Tāj al-Mukallal, 70, Ibn Shatti: Mukhtasar Tabaqāt al-Hanābila, hlm. 42.

Ketahuilah bahwa segala yang Allah tetapkan bagi manusia adalah untuk kemanfaatan mereka; baik untuk mendatangkan manfaat seperti keinginan terhadap makanan, maupun untuk mencegah keburukan seperti kemampuan untuk marah. Meski demikian, ketika keinginan terhadap makanan berlebihan, ia menjadi kerakusan dan karenanya merugikan. Demikian pula kemarahan yang tidak seimbang dalam diri seseorang menjadi sumber kerusakan.

Tujuan kitab ini adalah untuk menerapkan hukum kebenaran dan kelurusan melalui saluran akal dan hati, serta untuk mengekang hawa agar tidak menimbulkan keburukan, dan untuk mengobati hawa yang telah diikuti namun bertentangan dengan hukum kebenaran dan kelurusan. Untuk alasan inilah, saya membagi kitab ini menjadi tiga puluh bab.

Keutamaan Akal

tentang hakikat akal dan tempatnya, dan selanjutnya topik ini

telah dibahas secara luas. Selain itu banyak hadith yang diriwayatkan untuk menetapkan keutamaan akal. Sebagian di antaranya telah saya sebutkan dalam kitab saya "Dzamm al-Hawa" (Celaan terhadap Hawa Nafsu), oleh karena itu saya tidak akan mengulanginya di sini, melainkan saya akan menyebutkan beberapa pilihan:

M ANUSIA TELAH BERBEDA PENDAPAT MENGENAI hakikat

Keutamaan sesuatu diketahui dari buahnya, dan di antara buah akal adalah mengenal Sang Pencipta, Yang Maha Tinggi. Sebab akal telah merenungkan dalil-dalil [keberadaan Sang Pencipta] hingga ia mengenal-Nya, dan telah merenungkan dalil-dalil kebenaran para Nabi hingga ia mengakui kebenaran mereka. Akal telah mendorong ketaatan kepada Allah dan kepada para rasul-Nya. Ia telah merancang cara untuk memperoleh segala yang sulit didapatkan, dan ia menjinakkan hewan-hewan. Ia mengajarkan manusia untuk membangun kapal yang dengannya ia mencapai apa yang lautan telah halangi manusia dari mencapainya, dan memperdaya burung-burung laut hingga dapat diburu. Semua ini sementara mata manusia mengamati akibat-akibat dan bekerja sesuai dengan keselamatan

dan kebutuhan. Lebih jauh lagi, ia mengajarkan manusia untuk meninggalkan kehidupan dunia demi akhirat. Karenanya, manusia memiliki kemuliaan di atas hewan-hewan yang tidak dianugerahi akal, dan akallah yang menjadikan manusia layak menerima firman Allah dan perintah-perintah-Nya. Dengannya, manusia mencapai derajat tertinggi yang dapat dicapai jenisnya dari kebaikan dunia dan akhirat, baik dalam ilmu maupun amal.

Keutamaan-keutamaan ini sudah cukup tanpa perlu menjelaskan secara terperinci.

Celaan terhadap Hawa (Nafsu)

AWA ADALAH KECENDERUNGAN TABIAT SESEORANG terhadap apa yang disukainya, dan hal itu tidak seharusnya dicela ketika yang

dicari adalah sesuatu yang halal. Namun, ia patut dicela ketika seseorang berlebihan dalam mengikuti hawa. Meski demikian, ketika hawa dicela secara mutlak, itu karena sebagian besarnya adalah

terlarang, atau karena manusia biasanya menafsirkan kehalalan secara longgar, sehingga mereka tenggelam di dalamnya secara berlebihan.

Ketahuilah bahwa sebagian dari jiwa batin adalah intelektual, yang keutamaannya adalah hikmah dan cacatnya adalah kebodohan; sebagian darinya bersifat emosional, yang keutamaannya adalah keberanian dan cacatnya adalah kepengecutan; sebagian darinya bersifat syahwat, yang keutamaannya adalah kesucian diri dan cacatnya adalah hawa yang tak terkendali. Menampilkan kesabaran dalam menghadapi keburukan adalah kelebihan jiwa batin yang dengannya seseorang menanggung kebaikan dan keburukan. Oleh karena itu, barangsiapa yang tidak memiliki kesabaran dan membiarkan hawanya memimpin akalnya, maka ia telah menjadikan yang mengikuti sebagai yang diikuti dan yang dipimpin sebagai pemimpin. Maka sudah sepatutnya segala yang ia inginkan akan berbalik kepadanya, dan ia akan dirugikan dari tempat yang ia harapkan manfaatnya, ia akan bersedih oleh apa yang ia harapkan kebahagiaan darinya.

Sesungguhnya manusia dimuliakan di atas hewan-hewan dengan akal (kecerdasan) yang ditetapkan untuk mengekang seseorang dari mengikuti hawa, oleh karena itu ketika seseorang tidak menerima keputusan akalnya dan justru tunduk pada keputusan hawanya, maka hewan menjadi lebih baik darinya. Di antara tanda-tanda yang membuktikan keutamaan menentang hawa adalah penghormatan dan keunggulan anjing pemburu atas anjing-anjing lainnya, yang disebabkan oleh kemampuan mereka menentang hawanya dan menjaga hasil buruannya untuk tuannya karena takut akan hukuman atau sebagai bentuk penghargaan.

Ketahuilah bahwa perumpamaan hawa adalah seperti air yang mengalir deras yang menghanyutkan perahu tabiat seseorang. Orang yang berakal sehat harus memahami bahwa menanggung kesulitan ketika menentang hawanya jauh lebih ringan daripada menanggung akibat dari mengikutinya; sebab yang paling ringan diharapkan ketika seseorang mengikuti hawanya adalah berada dalam keadaan di mana ia tidak dapat menikmatinya dan tidak pula memiliki kemampuan untuk mencegah dirinya dari mengikutinya, karena terbiasa dengan sesuatu membuatnya menjadi kecanduan; seperti mereka yang kecanduan hubungan seksual atau minuman keras.

Merenungkan perkara-perkara ini memudahkan seseorang untuk menolak hawanya. Di antara hal yang menjadikan hawa tercela di mata seseorang adalah perenungan mendalam terhadap dirinya sendiri, yang darinya ia akan menyimpulkan bahwa ia tidak diciptakan untuk bertindak sesuai hawanya. Sebab unta makan lebih banyak darinya, burung berkopulasi lebih sering darinya, dan nafsu hewan-hewan tidak terbatas serta mereka tidak mengalami penyesalan sesudahnya. Maka ketika [mengetahui bahwa] bagian manusia dari nafsu tidak pernah sempurna, dan ketika nafsu-nafsu itu dicemari oleh ketidaksempurnaan [manusia], maka selayaknya manusia mengetahui bahwa [semua tanda ini menunjukkan bahwa] mereka tidak diciptakan untuk mengikuti hawa mereka sendiri, meskipun demikian, hawa yang tercela

Celaan terhadap Hawa (Nafsu)

adalah yang melampaui batas dan itulah yang dihukumi oleh akal sebagai cacat, sebagaimana telah saya jelaskan sebelumnya. Oleh karena itu, apa pun yang engkau inginkan yang memang ada kebutuhan untuk memilikinya dan yang membantu jiwa untuk menjadi lebih baik adalah terpuji, bukan tercela.

Perbedaan Antara Pandangan Akal dan Pandangan Hawa

tanpa mempertimbangkan akibat-akibatnya,

meskipun seseorang mungkin tahu bahwa kepuasan ini akan membawa gangguan yang justru melebihinya [melebihi kepuasan itu] dan juga menghalanginya dari memperoleh kepuasan serupa lainnya. Namun hawa memalingkan seseorang dari merenungkan semua ini, yang menjadikannya turun ke derajat hewan, meskipun hewan dimaafkan karena mereka tidak memperhatikan akibat dari apa yang mereka lakukan. Oleh karena itu, orang yang bijak tidak seharusnya turun dari kedudukan tinggi dan mulia yang ia dimuliakan dengannya, ke kedudukan makhluk yang rendah [hewan]. Akal harus memperhatikan akibat-akibat [dari perbuatan], seseorang harus meneliti manfaat dan kepentingan, karena akal bertindak sebagai penasihat yang tegas dan dokter yang memberi nasihat.

K ETAHUILAH BAHWA HAWA MENGAJAK UNTUK MERAIH kenik-

Perumpamaan hawa adalah seperti anak kecil yang bodoh dan orang sakit yang rakus. Untuk alasan ini, ketika akal menangkap sesuatu yang berbeda dari ajakan hawa, maka orang yang bijak dan berakal seharusnya bermusyawarah dengan akalnya, terlebih karena ia

Perbedaan Antara Pandangan Akal dan Pandangan...

menyadari bahwa akal itu berpengetahuan dan tulus dalam nasihatnya. Ia harus bersabar atas apa yang diperintahkan akal kepadanya, karena mengetahui keutamaan akal sudah cukup baginya untuk mengutamakannya. Lebih jauh, jika ia membutuhkan bukti tambahan tentang kelurusan keputusan akal, hendaklah ia merenungkan akibat-akibat mengikuti hawanya seperti tersingkapnya aib di hadapan manusia, tercela, kehilangan keutamaan dan amal kebaikan. Sebab pernahkah kemuliaan merosot, kehormatan dihinakan dan direndahkan, atau burung diburu kecuali sebagai akibat dari mengikuti hawa?!

Buktinya juga dapat ditemukan ketika seseorang mengantisipasi apa yang hawanya ajakkan sebelum meraihnya. Ia harus merenungkan keadaannya setelah kepuasannya berlalu, ia harus membandingkan antara kepuasannya dan dosanya, karena saat itulah ia akan mengetahui bahwa ia telah kehilangan dua kali lipat dari apa yang ia peroleh.

Seorang penyair berkata mengenai hal ini:

Betapa banyak kepuasan yang memicu kegembiraan, Berakhir dengan mengungkap kesedihan dan duka

Betapa banyak nafsu yang merampas dari pemiliknya Pakaian agama dan keutamaan

Ketahuilah bahwa ketika manusia bertindak sesuai dengan hawanya, ia akan menemukan dalam dirinya kehinaan dan kerendahan, meskipun hawa ini tidak merugikannya, karena ia merasa ditundukkan oleh hawanya. Namun ketika ia mengalahkan hawanya, ia akan menemukan dalam dirinya kemuliaan dan kebanggaan karena ia merasa menang dan berjaya. Untuk alasan ini, ketika orang-orang melihat seorang zahid, mereka takjub kepadanya, dan mereka mencium tangannya [sebagai tanda hormat] karena mereka memandangnya sebagai orang yang cukup kuat untuk meninggalkan apa yang mereka lemah menghadapinya, yaitu menentang hawa.

Menghindari Cinta yang Membara ('Ishq)

baik pada tubuh, agama, atau keduanya; sebuah penyakit yang untuknya

saya telah menyusun kitab 'Dzamm al-Hawa', di mana saya telah mencantumkan cukup banyak obat untuk 'ishq. Meskipun demikian, saya akan menyebutkan sebagian dari obat-obat ini di sini agar tidak melewatkan apa pun yang menjadi tujuan kitab ini, oleh karena itu saya berkata:

'I SHQ ADALAH PENYAKIT YANG TELAH MENGHANCURKAN banyak orang

Barangsiapa yang menahan diri dari percampuran [unsur-unsur berbahaya yang menyebabkan penyakit] dengan menundukkan pandangannya, akan selamat dari penyakit ini. Jika tidak, ia akan sama sakitnya dengan orang yang mencampurkan diri dalam perbuatan-perbuatan berbahaya tersebut.

Jika ia memperbaikinya sebelum mengakar dalam, obat mungkin bermanfaat baginya. Namun jika ia membiarkannya hingga mengakar dalam, obat tidak akan bermanfaat lagi. Ketahuilah bahwa sekadar memandang apa yang diinginkan tidak menyebabkan 'ishq, melainkan memandang yang diinginkan secara berulang-ulang yang semakin bertambah karena didukung dan ditopang oleh kekuatan keserakahan, masa muda, dan syahwat. Oleh karena itu, barangsiapa yang menginginkan obatnya, hendaklah ia bersegera sebelum penyakit ini mengakar dalam, yaitu dengan menutup jalan-jalan yang mengarah kepadanya (menundukkan pandangan)

Menghindari Cinta yang Membara ('Ishq)

dan dengan menanggungnya dengan sabar. Sebab sesungguhnya pengendalian diri dan keteguhan adalah sebaik-baik obat. Sebaik-baik penolong dalam hal ini adalah takwa kepada Allah, mengekang jiwa yang sombong dari keadaan kehinaan, dan mengingat cacat-cacat batin orang yang dicintai. Sebagaimana Ibn Mas'ud berkata: "Jika salah seorang dari kalian menyukai seorang wanita, hendaklah ia mengingat kekurangannya."
content_ar: % Fora more detailed biography of al-Hafiz Ibn al-Jawzi, refer to: Ibn Athir’s a/- Kamil, 12/71, Sibt Ibn al-Jawzi: Mirat al-Zamén, 8/481, al-Mundhisi: al-Takmila, Biography 608, al-Bagghal: a/-Mashyakha, 140, Aba Shamma: Thail al-Randatain, 21, Ibn al-Sai: a-Jami', 9/65, Ibn Khalkan: a/-Wafaydt, 3/140, al-Dhahabi: a/- ‘bar, 4/297, Diwal al-الإسلام, 2/19, Tathkirat al-Huffaz, 4/1342, Siyar al-A Var alNubula’, 21/365, Ibn Kathir: al-Bidayab wa’l-Nihayah, 13/26, Yon Rajab: Thail Tabagét al-Hanabila, 1/399, al-Jazati: Ghayat wa'l-Nihayab, 1/375, Siddiq حسن Khan: a/:Taj al Mukallal, 70, Ibn Shatti: Mukbtasar Tabagat al-Hanabila, p. 42. Know that all that الله has allocated for humankind is for the purpose of benefiting them; either to bring forth a benefit مثل the desire for food, or to prevent a harm مثل the capability of الغضب. That said, عندما the desire for food is in excess it becomes gluttony and ومن ثَم, harmful. On a similar note الغضب عندما imbalanced within a person becomes mischievous. The purpose of this الكتاب is to employ the law of soundness and correctness through the channels of the العقل and the القلب, and to constrain Hawa لذا that it does not cause harm, and to treat Hawa that has been acted upon yet opposes the law of soundness and correctness. For this العقل, I have divided this الكتاب into thirty الأبواب. The الفضيلة of the العقل of the العقل and its location , subsequently this subject has been extensively discussed. كذلك many hadiths have been narrated establishing the excellence of the العقل. Some of which I have already mentioned in my الكتاب “Dispraise of Desires”, لذلك I will not repeat them here rather I will mention a selection: P EOPLE HAVE DIFFERED فيما يخص the essence The excellence of a thing is known by its fruits, and from among the fruits of the العقل is knowing the Creator, may He be exalted. For the العقل has contemplated the proofs [of the existence of the Creator] حتى it knew Him, and has contemplated the proofs of the truthfulness of the الأنبياء حتى it recognized their truthfulness. The العقل has encouraged obedience to الله and to His الرسل. It has schemed to get all that is difficult to obtain, and it tamed the animals. It taught man to build ships on which he reached what the sea had prevented men from reaching, and tricked the birds of the sea حتى they were hunted. All of this بينما man’s eyes observe the consequences and work in accordance with safety and need. علاوة على ذلك it taught man to relinquish the الحياة of this الدنيا for الآخرة. لأن of it, human beings have excellence over animals that have been prevented from having it (the العقل), and it is what qualifies man for the speech of الله and His commandments. With it, man reaches the highest ranks that his kind can reach from the الخير of this الدنيا and الآخرة, in both العلم and actions. These الفضائل suffice the need to delve into detail. Dispraise of Hawa (desires) AWA IS THE INCLINATION OF one’s nature to what He: it and that should not be criticised عندما what is sought بعد is lawful. غير أن, it should be dispraised عندما one is excessive in following Hawa. That said, عندما Hawa is criticised in an absolute manner, it is لأن most of it is either impermissible, or لأن people usually interpret lawfulness, and ومن ثَم indulge in it in excess. Know that part of the inner self is intellectual, the الفضيلة of which is الحكمة, the vice of which is ignorance, part of it is elicited, the الفضيلة of which is poignancy, the vice of which is cowardice, part of it is lustful, the الفضيلة of which is العفة and the vice of which is unrestrained Hawa. Exhibiting الصبر in the face of vice is a merit of the inner self by which a person endures both goodness and الشر. لذلك, whoever lacks الصبر and allows his //awd to lead his العقل has then made the follower be followed and the led a leader. That said, it is expected that everything he desires will return to him and that he will be harmed from where he expected to benefit, he will be saddened by that which he expected happiness to come from. Indeed human beings are favored over beastly animals with the العقل (intelligence) that is designated to restrain one from following Hawa, لذلك عندما a person does not accept the judgment of his العقل and abides by the judgment of his 7Yawa, the beastly animal becomes bettet than him. From the indicative signs through which the excellence of contradicting Hawa is proven is the honoring and the superiority of hunting dogs over other dogs, which is بسبب their ability to contradict their Hawd and to keep what they hunt for their master out of الخوف of العقاب or as a show of appreciation. Know that the example of Haw is like rampant flowing water leading the ship of one’s nature. A person of sound العقل should comprehend, enduring الشدة عندما contradicting his Hawa is much easier, than enduring what comes from following it; for the least to be expected عندما one follows his Hand is being in a state in which one neither can enjoy it nor possess the ability to prevent oneself from following it, becoming accustomed to something allows it to become an addiction; مثل those addicted to sexual intercourse or alcohol. Contemplating these matters makes it easier for a person to reject his Hawa. From amongst what makes Hawa despicable to a person is the deep reflection of oneself, upon which one will deduce that he was not created to act in conformity with his Hawa. For a camel eats more than he does, a bird copulates more than him, and the desires of animals are unrestricted and they do not experience regret afterwards. وهكذا عندما [knowing that] the share of humans from desires is never ultimate, and عندما those desires were spoiled with [human’s] imperfections, وبناءً على ذلك humankind should know that [all these signs indicate that] they were not created للاتباع theit own Hawa, notwithstanding, the dispraised Dispraise of Hawa (desires) Hawa is, that which exceeds the limits and it is that which the العقل judges as faulty, as I have already elucidated. لذلك whatever you desire of which there is a necessity to possess and that which assists the self to improve is praised rather than dispraised. The Difference Between the Perspective of العقل and the Perspective of Hawa ure without taking into consideration its consequences, though one might know that this satisfaction will bring annoyance that actually exceeds it [exceeds the satisfaction] and أيضاً prevents one from attaining other similar satisfaction. Nonetheless Hawa turns one away from reflecting on all this, which makes one lower to the exact state of animals, غير أن animals are excused لأن they do not observe the consequences of what they do. ومن ثَم, a wise person should not descend from a high and noble state that he is honored with, to the state of those that are low [animals], the العقل should observe the consequences {of actions] ينبغي للمرء examine the benefits and interests, بما أن it acts as a resolute person and an advisory doctor. | q NOW THAT HAWA CALLS TO ATTAINING pleas- The similitude of Hand is that of an ignorant boy and a gluttonous sick person. For this العقل, عندما the العقل perceives something that differs to the Hawa’s entreaty, a wise and reasonable person should then consult his العقل, particularly as he is The Difference Between the Perspective of العقل and the Perspective... aware that it is knowledgeable as well sincere in its advice, he should be patient upon what the العقل orders him to do, لأن knowing the excellence of the العقل is enough for him to النعمة it. Further إذا he needs additional proof of the uprightness of the العقل’s decision, he should contemplate the consequences of following his Hawa مثل scandalous exposure in front of people, vilification, missing الفضائل and الخير deeds. For this glory has ever deteriorated, الشرف has been disgraced and humiliated or a bird hunted except ونتيجة لذلك of following Hawa?! The proof can أيضاً be found عندما a person anticipates what his Hawd is calling him to prior to attaining it, he should then reflect on his state بعد his satisfaction has passed, he should compare this between his satisfaction and his الذنب for that is عندما he will know that he has lost double of what he has gained. A poet said فيما يخص this: How much satisfaction that provoked happiness, Ended up revealing الحزن and sorrows How many desites ripped from their participants The garment of religion and الفضيلة Know that عندما a human acts in accordance to his Hawa, he will find in himself disgrace and humiliation, even إذا this Hawa does not harm him, that is لأن he feels subdued by his Hawa, but عندما he overcomes his Hawd he will find in himself الشرف and الكبر as he will feel triumphant and victorious. For this العقل, عندما people see an ascetic they become amazed by him, and they would kiss his hand [to show الاحترام] لأن they perceive him as a person who is strong enough to leave what they were weak in the face of and that is disobeying Hawa. Averting Passionate المحبة (Yshq) whether in their bodies, religion or both; a المرض for which Ihave composed the الكتاب ‘Dispravse of Hawa’,wherein I have listed enough cures for “shg, nonetheless, I will mention some of these cures here لذا as not to leave out anything that this الكتاب was intended for, لذلك, I say: I SHQ IS A المرض THAT HAS RUINED many people Whoever abstains from the amalgamation [of harmful elements causing المرض] by lowering his gaze, will be saved from this المرض, otherwise he will be as much diseased as he who amalgamates in such harmful acts. إذا he redresses this قبل it becomes deeply rooted, the الدواء might benefit him. غير أن إذا he leaves it حتى it becomes deeply rooted the الدواء will be of no benefit. Know that the mete looking at what is coveted does not result in %shg, rather looking at the coveted often increases as it is aided and supported by the القوة of الطمع, youthfulness and lust. لذلك whoever wants the الدواء should hasten to it قبل this المرض becomes deeply rooted and that is by blocking the means leading to it (lowering the gaze) Averting Passionate المحبة (‘Ishq) and by enduring it with الصبر. For indeed self-restraint and القوة are the best of cures. The best of aids in this is الخوف of الله, restraining the disdainful self from the state of humiliation and remembering the inner flaws of the loved one. As Ibn Mas‘td said: “إذا any of you like a woman, he should remember her flaws.”
